_meta.philosophy: Challenging but not impossible. Fun but not trivial. The game is about perseverance under persecution — combat should feel like a struggle that rewards faith and wisdom, not just grinding. Death should feel meaningful, not frustrating.
_meta.reference: FF6 damage formulas, Chrono Trigger encounter pacing, Octopath difficulty curves

difficulty_modes._note: Selectable at game start and changeable from the menu at any time. No shame in changing — even the saints needed help.

pilgrim.name: Pilgrim
pilgrim.description: For those who want to walk the road and hear the story. Enemies are gentler, resources more plentiful. The journey matters more than the battle.
pilgrim.subtitle: Walk in peace
pilgrim._design_note: Story mode. You can still die if you're reckless, but the game guides you through. Ideal for people who love JRPGs for narrative, not min-maxing.

disciple.name: Disciple
disciple.description: The intended experience. Enemies test your resolve. Resources require stewardship. Prayer is not optional — it is survival.
disciple.subtitle: Take up your cross
disciple._design_note: The real game. Balanced for a player who uses all systems — miracles, items, guard, virtue scaling. Bosses require strategy. Random encounters require attention.

martyr.name: Martyr
martyr.description: For those who seek the cross. Enemies are relentless. Resources are scarce. Every battle is a test of faith. The Accuser shows no mercy.
martyr.subtitle: Be faithful unto death
martyr._design_note: Hard mode. Bosses gain extra attack patterns. The Accuser's spiritual attacks last longer. Healing is weaker. This is for players who want the martyrdom to feel earned.

saint.name: Saint
saint.description: The game fights back. Enemies use advanced tactics. Bosses have new phases. The Accuser remembers everything.
saint.subtitle: Through the fire
saint._design_note: Unlocked via Divine Recycling after completing Martyr difficulty. Enemies gain new abilities, bosses have extra phases.

apostle.name: Apostle
apostle.description: The final cycle. No mercy. No safety net. Every enemy is at full power. One death per book is permanent.
apostle.subtitle: Sent forth into the fire
apostle._design_note: The ultimate challenge. Completing this unlocks the True Ending. The Accuser remembers every choice across all cycles.

xp_curve.formula: Phase 1 (1-30): 20 * level^1.65 | Phase 2 (31-60): phase1_cap + 45*(level-30)^1.75 | Phase 3 (61-100): phase2_cap + 80*(level-60)^1.85
xp_curve._note: XP needed to reach level N from level N-1. Slow start, steeper later but never grindy. Book Zero should take the player from level 1 to roughly 8-10.

stat_growth._note: Per-level stat gains for Usagi. Growth is not flat — it follows a curve with two sweet spots (levels 5-8 and 15-20) separated by a plateau. This creates the classic JRPG feel where certain level-ups feel like breakthroughs.
stat_growth._note_spr: Spirit grows fastest because Usagi is a pilgrim, not a warrior. His strength is prayer, not the blade.

cumulative_stats_at_key_levels._note: What Usagi looks like at key story moments

novice.description: Learning the road. Book Zero through Book II.

veteran.description: Walking with purpose. Books III through VI.

saint.description: The final ascent. Books VII, VIII, and the return.

damage_formulas._source: Adapted from FF6 with modifications for virtue scaling

physical_attack._note: FF6-style: attack scales quadratically so level-ups feel impactful. Defense also quadratic so high-defense enemies require holy damage or strategy, not just grinding.

holy_damage._note: Spirit-based, boosted by faith virtue. Ignores physical defense — holy damage bypasses armor. This is why prayer matters.

healing._note: Charity makes healing stronger — CCC 1827 in mechanical form.

critical_hit._note: 6% base + 3% per prudence level. At prudence 5, that's 21% crit rate. Prudence rewards.

encounter_tables._note: Each area has an encounter table. Encounters are grouped into tiers matching expected player level ranges. The engine selects from the appropriate tier based on player level.

encounter_rate._note: Average ~1 encounter every 48-64 steps. Safe zones have zero encounters — sacred spaces are sacred.

tier_1.description: Early encounters. Teach basic combat. Player should win in 2-4 rounds.

tier_2.description: Mid encounters. Require use of items and prayer. 3-6 rounds.

tier_3.description: Late encounters. Full strategy needed. Guard timing matters.

mini_boss.description: Area guardians. Multiple attack patterns. Require miracle use.

boss.description: Story bosses. Multi-phase. Unique mechanics per boss.

balance_targets._note: How long fights should last, measured in player turns
balance_targets.boss: 15-25 turns, multi-phase, uses miracles + items + guard
balance_targets.accuser: Cannot be killed. Survive X turns while resisting spiritual attacks.

pacing_rules.safe_rest_before_boss: Always a save point and rest area before a boss
pacing_rules.post_boss_breathing_room: Reduced encounter rate for 1 area after boss
pacing_rules.accuser_encounters: Once per act (4 per book). Always preceded by story buildup.
pacing_rules.difficulty_spike_warning: If player dies 3 times in same area, offer to reduce difficulty

ai_behavior_patterns._note: Enemy AI templates. Each enemy uses a pattern set with weighted actions. Smarter enemies shift weights based on player state.

aggressive.description: Hits hard, rarely guards. Soldiers, beasts.

defensive.description: Turtles up when hurt. Armored enemies.

tactical.description: Uses status effects and weaknesses. Officers, sorcerers.

spiritual.description: The Accuser's pattern. All attacks are spiritual status effects.

environmental.description: Not a creature. Fire, smoke, falling objects.

pack_hunter.description: Stronger in groups. Flees when last survivor.

adaptive_rules.when_player_low_hp: Aggressive enemies become more aggressive (+20% attack weight)
adaptive_rules.when_player_guarding: Tactical enemies use debuffs instead of attacks
adaptive_rules.when_player_praying: Aggressive enemies try to interrupt (attack during cast)
adaptive_rules.when_alone: Pack hunters flee. Solo enemies become desperate (heavier attacks).
adaptive_rules.martyr_mode_extra: Bosses gain 1 additional ability. Enemies coordinate better.

post_battle_regen._note: No post-battle regen. Resources matter. This is persecution, not a picnic.

save_points._note: Save points do NOT heal. They're a bookmark, not a rest stop. Rest at sacred sites.

flee_system._note: Fleeing is always an option for random encounters. No shame in running from soldiers — discretion is a virtue (CCC 1806: prudence).

divine_recycling._doctrine: God wastes nothing. Every prayer, every step, every wound is remembered and transformed. Divine Recycling is not repetition — it is refinement.
